content:
{
  "title": "Counterfactual Prompt Augmentation for Bias Mitigation in LLMs",
  "problem": "Large language models (LLMs) often generate outputs that reflect social biases and stereotypes present in their training data. Reducing such biases during inference remains a challenging open problem.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs see increasing deployment in high-stakes settings, mitigating bias is critical for fairness and trust. Prompt engineering has shown early promise for bias reduction, but current methods are insufficiently robust, especially for subtle or intersectional biases. There is a pressing need for prompting techniques that systematically expose and counteract LLM biases in real-time.",
  "method": "We propose a novel counterfactual prompt augmentation method: for each input prompt, automatically generate a set of minimally-different counterfactual prompts (e.g., swapping protected attributes, altering gendered language, or changing cultural context) while preserving semantic intent. The LLM is asked to answer all prompts in parallel, and a bias-aware response aggregator selects or synthesizes an output that is invariant across counterfactuals or explicitly de-biases stereotyped content. This technique leverages the model's context window to force consideration of alternative perspectives and reduce reliance on stereotypical associations.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on established bias benchmarks (e.g., StereoSet, CrowS-Pairs) and real-world QA tasks, comparing our method to standard prompting and instruction-tuned baselines. Metrics include stereotype score reduction, answer consistency across counterfactuals, and human-rated fairness. Ablations will assess the impact of aggregator strategies and number of counterfactuals."
}
